title: periclymenum vulgare var. quercifolium
intro: Periclymenum vulgare var. quercifolium, also known as the oak-leaved woodbine, is a variety of the common woodbine plant native to Europe. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which can help to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The medicinal properties of oak-leaved woodbine can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which have been shown to have antioxidant, antimicrobial, and immunomodulatory effects. Oak-leaved woodbine can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been traditionally used to help alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and sore throats, as well as to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
